t_ch: 小鸟又恢复了那副*贱贱*的神情——说真的，它是你见过最不讨喜的卡通形象了，没有之一。
t_en: The bird regains its *sly* expression — honestly, it's the most unpleasant cartoon character you've ever seen, hands down.

t_ch: （软的不行…就别怪我来硬的了！）
t_en: (If a good cop won't work... guess I'll have to go bad cop then!)

t_ch: 钟表把戏，强制启动！
t_en: Clockwork, forced activation!

t_ch: …等等，喂，你要干什么？！
t_en: ...Wait, hey, what're you doing!?

t_ch: 计划还得从长计议…
t_en: We need to plan this out carefully...

t_ch: 小鸟又恢复了那副*贱贱*的神情——说真的，它是你见过最不讨喜的卡通形象了，没有之一。
t_en: The bird regains its *sly* expression — honestly, it's the most unpleasant cartoon character you've ever seen, hands down.

t_ch: （软的不行…就别怪我来硬的了！）
t_en: (If a good cop won't work... guess I'll have to go bad cop then!)

t_ch: 钟表把戏，强制启动！
t_en: Clockwork, forced activation!

t_ch: …等等，喂，你要干什么？！
t_en: ...Wait, hey, what're you doing!?

t_ch: 计划还得从长计议…
t_en: We need to plan this out carefully...

t_ch: 好心的客人，请问你有看到一只折纸小鸟吗？
t_en: Kind guest, might I ask if you've seen an origami bird?

t_ch: 一只身体圆滚滚，羽毛滑溜溜，和我们长得一模一样的折纸小鸟？啾啾？
t_en: A rotund, ruffled, origami bird that looks just like us? Tweet tweet?

t_ch: 我面前就有四只。
t_en: I see four standing before me.

t_ch: 不一样，不一样！那孩子的羽毛是蓝色的，啾。
t_en: They're not the same! That child has blue feathers, tweet.

t_ch: 折纸小鸟叼来树枝，修理大树，筑造巢穴。大树的迷宫弯弯绕绕，只有小鸟才知道树叶间的捷径，啾。
t_en: Origami birds pick up branches, repair the Great Tree, and build nests. The Great Tree's maze is winding, and only birds know the shortcuts between leaves, tweet.

t_ch: 但可怜的孩子，它还没学会找到回家的路，就在捉迷藏时走丢了……
t_en: But this poor child hasn't learned the way home, and it got lost in the labyrinth...

t_ch: 拜托了，客人！请你帮忙去找找那孩子吧，啾！
t_en: Please, guest! Help look for that child, tweet!

t_ch: 桌上堆着许多建筑的绘画草稿。笔触简略，但已然能看清画面中多座宏伟且奇幻的建筑。
t_en: The table is strewn with many architectural concept drawings. Their lines are simple, but you can clearly see the many grandiose and fantastical buildings in the drawings.

t_ch: 某人在建筑旁写下一行小字：「十二时刻•设计草图」
t_en: Someone has written something beside the building: Twelve Hours Blueprint.

t_ch: 书架上放着一本名为《梦境施工指南》的手账册，上面字迹杂乱，似乎有不少人同时在使用。
t_en: On the bookshelf is a handbook named Dreamscape Construction Guide. The scribbling on it is haphazard, like many people were perusing it at the same time.

t_ch: 扉页上一行字尤为醒目：「筑梦千万条，有趣第一条！」
t_en: A line stands out on the title page: "There are countless guidelines to dreamweaving, but making it interesting is the top priority!"

t_ch: 桌上放着一张崭新的《逐梦客时讯》，头条处的大字印着这样一条报道。
t_en: A glossy new Dreamchaser Bulletin is placed on the desk, with the following header printed in huge letters.

t_ch: 「琥珀历<unbreak>2151</unbreak>纪，筹备已久的『筑梦工程』正式启动，预计于本琥珀纪内完成『十二梦境』的建设。目前，一期工程『白日梦酒店』已开始动工。」
t_en: "<unbreak>2151</unbreak> AE: The long-prepared Dreamweaving Project has officially launched. The Twelve Dreamscapes are slated to be completed within this Amber Era. Currently, phase one of the Reverie Hotel's construction has commenced."

t_ch: 「该工程将由隐夜鸫家系全权负责。根据洽谈结果，家主格拉克斯表示没有加入『家族』的意向，但愿意支持家族提出的梦境开发计划……」
t_en: "The Nightingale Family is fully responsible for this project. According to negotiations, their head, Glaux, has expressed no intention to join The Family, but is willing to support the Dreamscape development plan that The Family has suggested..."

t_ch: 「与此同时，隐夜鸫向银河发出邀约——欢迎宇宙中的建筑家与忆质学者来到匹诺康尼，共筑美梦世界……」
t_en: "Concurrently, the Nightingales have also extended an invitation to the universe — to welcome the architects and memoria scholars of the cosmos to build this world of Dreamjoy together..."

t_ch: ——没听过来工地还要带孩子的！
t_en: ...I've never heard of anyone who brings their kids to the construction site!

t_ch: 一边设计建筑，一边钻研难搞的忆质，还要被梦里的怪物追着跑…「白日梦酒店」好不容易开工，结果又被这小孩儿耽误进度！
t_en: Designing a structure, studying how to work with this finicky memoria, and being hunted by in-dream monsters... Work on the Reverie has finally started, only to have this child impeding our progress once again!

t_ch: 告诉教授，我不干了，我要跑路！
t_en: Tell the professor I'm done, I'm gone!

t_ch: 哎呀，冯恩先生，清醒点吧——你的大名还挂在星际和平公司的黑名单呢，除了那位教授，宇宙中没人敢接你的简历。
t_en: Oh Mr. Vaughn, please be realistic. Your name is still blacklisted by the IPC. Apart from the professor, no one in the universe will hire you.

t_ch: 更何况…那孩子也不是自愿留在这片梦境中的，你就体谅一下吧。而且，他很可爱呀~
t_en: Furthermore... it's not like that child wants to be in this Dreamscape of his own accord, so please be more understanding. Also, isn't he super cute?

t_ch: 就因为他年龄小？凭什么，我也是小孩儿！
t_en: Just because he's young? How's that fair? I'm a child too!

t_ch: 在梦里不意味着能说梦话，冯恩先生，你可是咱们里最年长的那位。
t_en: Just because you're in a dream, doesn't mean you can spit delusions, Mr. Vaughn. You're the oldest among us.

t_ch: 据我所知，那孩子在来到隐夜鸫家系前，是一个流浪的星际难民。
t_en: From what I know, that child was a wandering interstellar refugee before he came to the Nightingale Family.

t_ch: 他没有亲人，没有家庭，也没受过正规的教育，更不知道该怎样正确地「接触他人」。
t_en: He had no kin, no family, and was never raised properly, much less how to interact with people.

t_ch: 或许…他是将冯恩先生看做同龄人，在别扭地示好呢？
t_en: Perhaps... he saw Mr. Vaughn as someone of his age, and was expressing affection, albeit inappropriately?
Hmph...

t_ch: 让我原谅他，除非他亲自对我的「冯恩三世美食加工机」道歉——犯错了，最少也得有句「对不起」吧？
t_en: For me to forgive him, he'd have to personally apologize to my Vaughn III Gourmet Refinement Machine. Shouldn't a "sorry" at least be in order when a mistake is made?

t_ch: 小筑梦师，你来评评——是不是这个道理！
t_en: Little Dreamweaver, you be the judge. Is that fair or not?

t_ch: 你说的没错！
t_en: You're right!

t_ch: 看见没？连刚入行的后辈都知道什么叫礼貌。
t_en: See? Even a junior who's just joined the industry knows etiquette.

t_ch: 或许他也有难言之隐…
t_en: Maybe there's something he can't tell us...

t_ch: 哎！你们怎么一个个都站他那边！
t_en: Bah! Why're you all taking his side!

t_ch: 呵呵，冯恩先生的心态确实像他的同龄人呢……
t_en: Heh-heh, Mr. Vaughn certainly acts like he's of the same age as the child...

t_ch: 不过…这孩子犯错了就喜欢躲起来，现在又藏去哪儿了呢？
t_en: But... this child likes hiding after making a mistake. Where could he have gone this time?

t_ch: {F#小妹妹}{M#小弟弟}，得麻烦你跟我们一起找找他了。
t_en: {F#Young lady}{M#Young man}, I'd have to trouble you to help look for him.

t_ch: 哼，他刚刚跑去走廊的另一头了，先过去看看吧。
t_en: Hmph, he ran off in the other direction of the corridor. Go look there first.

t_ch: 「白日梦酒店」刚开始动工，希望他不要闯进什么危险的地方。
t_en: The Reverie's construction just started. I hope he doesn't barge into anywhere dangerous.

t_ch: 只有巴罗那种笨蛋才会明知故犯。这小子应该没这么傻…吧？
t_en: Only that fool Barlow would knowingly do something bad. This kid wouldn't be that stupid... right?

t_ch: 难说。师父手下的筑梦师可没几个正常人。
t_en: Hard to say. Most Dreamweavers working under master aren't completely sane.

t_ch: 哈哈，你说的也是，毕竟…咱俩也不怎么正常。
t_en: Haha, you're right. I mean... neither of us is normal either.

t_ch: 脑子正常的哪愿意在这么危险的地方干活啊？每天除了闷头画稿、钻进梦里施工——甚至还要被人诬陷诽谤。
t_en: Who would want to work in such a dangerous place if they didn't have a screw loose? With heads buried in architectural drafts, working deep in the dream, and even subjected to accusations and slander.

t_ch: 「乔伊女士，您对『白日梦酒店』的设计与艾普瑟隆的某处建筑十分相似，您对此有何解释吗？」——哇，我听着都要吐了。你这都能忍？
t_en: "Lady Joey, your design for the Reverie Hotel looks highly similar to a certain building in Epsilon. Do you have an explanation for this?" Sheesh, just listening to this tripe makes me want to puke. You can tolerate this?

t_ch: 一点闲话罢了，常听常新，算不上什么大事。
t_en: It's just some chatter. You get used to it after a while, it's nothing major.

t_ch: 不过…也只有在梦中，我们那些被人称为荒诞的想法才有可能实现。冯恩先生，你不也是为此而来吗？
t_en: But... it's only in a dream that our supposedly hare-brained concepts can be realized. Mr. Vaughn, didn't you come for this very reason too?

t_ch: 哼哼，你说的也是，毕竟…咱俩也算不上什么正常人啊。
t_en: Heh-heh, you're right, I mean... neither of us is anywhere close to normal.

t_ch: 呜呜…我的「冯恩三世」…好不容易找到了点在梦里加工美食的窍门…
t_en: *sob* My Vaughn III... I finally managed to get the hang of making snacks in a dream too...

t_ch: 那帮老古董非说这又是什么爆炸装备——不过就是外形有那么一点点与众不同嘛…
t_en: That antiquated bunch claimed this was something explosive... It just looks a bit odd is all...

t_ch: 他们都不敢尝一口「冯恩三世」做出来的点心！
t_en: They don't even dare try a bite of the snacks Vaughn III made!

t_ch: 醒醒吧。就算没那孩子捣乱，也没人敢用你发明的东西做饭吃。
t_en: Wake up. Even if that child wasn't causing a fuss, no one would dare to eat anything you make.

t_ch: 上一个这么大胆的同事还躺在医疗室里呢。
t_en: The last person who was that brave is still lying in the clinic.

t_ch: 哟，忆质重构、模因合成、光锥材料…这些忆庭的玩意儿居然真让他给搭出来了。可以啊，这小子从哪学的筑梦技巧？
t_en: Woah, memoria refactoring, memetic synthesis, Light Cone materials... He actually constructed all these concepts that existed only in the Garden of Recollection. Not too shabby. Where'd this kid learn his dreamweaving techniques?

t_ch: 大概是在我们工作的时候吧，毕竟他一直躲在边上看着。
t_en: Probably while we were working. He's always been lurking by the side watching us work.

t_ch: 我原来以为这孩子只是好奇，施工相关的讨论也没有刻意避着他…没想到他都记住了。
t_en: I thought this child was just curious, so I didn't keep him out of earshot during our architectural conversations... Turns out, he memorized it all.

t_ch: …或许，这孩子比我们想象的都能干。
t_en: ...Maybe this child is more capable than we thought.

t_ch: 别瞎想了。现在咋办？直接撬门？
t_en: We need to focus. What now? Do we break the door open?

t_ch: 乔伊摇了摇头，她弯下腰，轻柔地敲了敲门板。房内传来窸窸窣窣的响动。
t_en: Joey shakes her head, bends down, and softly knocks on the door. A rustling can be heard from behind it.

t_ch: 虽然手法还比较稚嫩，但空间与结构都很稳定，你造出了一处非常优秀的「庇护所」。
t_en: The craftsmanship is amateur, but the space and structure are stable. You've made an outstanding shelter.

t_ch: 孩子，你是在哪学到的筑梦技巧？
t_en: Child, where did you learn these dreamweaving skills?

t_ch: …怎么，又要把我抓起来吗？
t_en: ...What, are you going to arrest me again?

t_ch: 不，是格拉克斯教授向你发出了邀请——请你正式加入「隐夜鸫」，成为一位筑梦师。
t_en: No, I'm here because Professor Glaux extended an invitation to you — to officially join the Nightingale Family as a Dreamweaver.

t_ch: 你是个敏锐的孩子，熟悉梦境，也拥有构筑梦境的天赋。教授不会拒绝这样的人才的。
t_en: You're sharp, familiar with the Dreamscape, and possess a talent for building Dreamscapes. The Professor would never refuse such a prodigy.

t_ch: …为、为什么？我不明白，我不是刚刚犯了错……
t_en: ...Wh—Why? I don't understand. I did something wrong...

t_ch: 我相信冯恩先生也只是想要句道歉而已——对吧？
t_en: I believe Mr. Vaughn just wants an apology... right?
...Hmph!

t_ch: 半透明的梦泡在乔伊的指尖凝结，变化成小鸟的形状，飞向门内。另一侧传来惊呼。
t_en: The translucent dream bubble coalesces on Joey's fingertip, turns into the shape of a bird, and flies through the door. A gasp can be heard from the other end.

t_ch: 你看，筑梦与诗歌很像，解析现有的结构，再搭建新的结构，创造优美而精巧的作品。我们擅长的就是利用混沌，再重塑其形体——
t_en: Look, dreamweaving is similar to poetry or songwriting: Analyzing the present structure, then reconstructing it to create an elegant and exquisite masterpiece. Our proficiency lies in reshaping chaos into other forms...

t_ch: 对你来说，{RUBY_B#匹诺康尼}这里{RUBY_E#}一定是个没有秩序，蛮不讲理的地方……
t_en: To you, {RUBY_B#Penacony}this{RUBY_E#} must be an orderless, reasonless place...

t_ch: 但总有一天，这里将会成为一片真正的美梦——
t_en: But one day, this place will become a real Dreamjoy...

t_ch: 我以隐夜鸫之名向你保证。
t_en: I promise you, in the name of the Nightingales.

t_ch: 不用急着做决定，慢慢考虑就好~
t_en: Don't worry about making a decision now, consider it carefully!

t_ch: 还没好好自我介绍过呢。我叫乔伊，隐夜鸫家系的筑梦学家，也是这处白日梦酒店的主设计师。
t_en: I haven't properly introduced myself. I am Joey, a Dreamweaver scholar of the Nightingale Family and the chief designer of the Reverie Hotel.

t_ch: 本大爷是银河第一的大发明家，稀世难遇的建筑天才——你可以叫我冯恩大人！
t_en: I'm the greatest inventor in the cosmos, a once-in-a-millennia architectural genius — you may call me Sir Vaughn!

t_ch: …乔伊，冯恩…我记住了。
t_en: ...Joey, Vaughn... I've got it.

t_ch: 隐夜鸫家系中有很多人，大家是因为热爱而聚在一起的逐梦者。
t_en: There are many in the Nightingale Family who are Dreamchasers, rallied together over a common passion.

t_ch: 也希望…我们能成为你的「家人」。
t_en: And we hope... that we will become your family.

t_ch: 孩子，你叫什么？
t_en: What's your name, child?

t_ch: ——<color=#dbc291ff>奥尔拉</color>。我的名字，就叫我奥尔拉吧。
t_en: ...<color=#dbc291ff>Orla</color>. My name... just call me Orla.

t_ch: 终于找到你了。
t_en: I've finally found you.

t_ch: 我想起来了…奥尔拉，我真正的名字。
t_en: I remember now... Orla is my real name.

t_ch: 之后我去了隐夜鸫，筑梦师接纳了我，教我知识，带我和他们一起搭建梦境……
t_en: Later, I went to the Nightingales, and the Dreamweavers took me in, taught me everything, and brought me along with them to build the Dreamscape...

t_ch: 而我有世界上最好的家人。温柔的乔伊、不着调的冯恩，还有巴罗和费歇尔…是他们教我关于筑梦的一切……
t_en: And I had the world's best family. The gentle Joey, the eccentric Vaughn, and Barlow and Fischer... They taught me everything about dreamweaving...

t_ch: 我也看到了。
t_en: I saw that too.

t_ch: 他们与历史记载的不太一样。
t_en: They are quite different from how they were recorded in history.

t_ch: 历史只写上了他们的名字，却没有记录下真正的他们…为什么？
t_en: History only wrote their names, but not their true selves... Why?

t_ch: 我记得，我们之间还有一个约定。我在流梦礁等待他们完成承诺，却一直没人来找我……
t_en: I recall that we had another promise among us. I waited for them at Dreamflux Reef to fulfill that promise, but no one ever came...

t_ch: 必须去看看，「过去」到底发生了什么…？
t_en: I must take a look. What exactly happened in the past...?

t_ch: 筑梦师巴罗，猎犬已经找到你的罪证了——勾连偷渡者、窃取家族机密、威胁匹诺康尼的和平…
t_en: Dreamweaver Barlow, the Bloodhounds have found proof of your crimes — consorting with stowaways, stealing Family secrets, and threatening Penacony's peace...

t_ch: 每一项罪名都足以让你重回监狱，走，跟我去公馆进行审讯吧！
t_en: Each crime is enough to send you back to jail. Come with me to the Pavilion to receive judgment!

t_ch: 呵，你们猎犬可真是威风，拿着橡木刚刚颁布的法令，就能有办法给我安上罪名。
t_en: Heh, you Hounds sure are cocky — you found a way to pin these crimes on me with the warrant that the Oaks just issued.

t_ch: 哼，你原本就是监狱中的囚犯，是「家族」为你担保，让你获得改过自新的机会，是你不珍惜！
t_en: Hmph, you were a prisoner to begin with. The Family vouched for you and gave you a new lease on life. But you didn't treasure it!

t_ch: 我不珍惜？哈…哈哈……真是一群走狗！
t_en: I didn't treasure it? Ha... Haha... What a bunch of lapdogs!

t_ch: 为我担保？笑话！把我从监狱中捞出来的是格拉克斯老头子——窃取机密，威胁和平？更是放屁！「隐夜鸫」的筑梦技术什么时候变成你们家族的东西了？
t_en: Vouch for me? What a joke! It was old man Glaux who bailed me out of prison. Stealing secrets, threatening peace? More wild accusations! When did the Nightingales' dreamweaving methods belong to you?

t_ch: 用人的时候多殷勤，呵呵…惊梦剧团都没你们翻脸快。
t_en: And you were so hospitable when you were using me, heh-heh... Even the Dreamjolt Troupe should learn from you when it comes to betrayal.

t_ch: 逮捕你，当然是为了梦境的安全……
t_en: Arresting you is for the sake of the Dreamscape's safety, of course...

t_ch: 被迷因袭击的乔伊，在实验中失踪的冯恩，还有被你们打压的费歇尔…这些都是为了「安全」？
t_en: Joey was attacked by memes, Vaughn went missing during an experiment, and Fischer was being muzzled by you lot... Was that for safety too?

t_ch: 这片梦境发展至此，皈依家族已经是大势所趋。梦境需要的是平稳、繁荣，这里容不下你这种爱惹是非的异端——投降吧！
t_en: The Family's governance is what contributed to this Dreamscape's development. The Dreamscape needs stability and prosperity, not radicals like you who sow chaos. Surrender!

t_ch: 哼，要是我拒绝呢？你如果对我使用暴力，那可是猎犬对隐夜鸫的冒犯。违背「五大家系联合一心」的宗旨，你们的家主担得起这个责任吗？
t_en: Hmph, and what if I refuse? If you detain me using violence, that's a slight on the Nightingales, a breach of the creed of the "unity of the five major lineages." Will your family head be able to bear this responsibility?

t_ch: 呵，我们当然不会违背条令…但不属于任何家系的忆域迷因可就不一定这么想了！
t_en: Heh, of course we wouldn't defy orders... but a Memory Zone Meme that does not belong to any Family wouldn't think that way!

t_ch: （坏了，那个人有危险……）
t_en: (Oh no, that person is in danger...)

t_ch: …咳、咳咳…多亏了你，总算赶跑他们了……
t_en: *cough* ...Thanks, you got rid of them...

t_ch: 谢谢你的帮助，你是…？
t_en: Thank you for your help, you are...?

t_ch: 我是奥尔拉的朋友。
t_en: I'm Orla's friend.

t_ch: 普通路过的好心人。
t_en: Just someone who happened to be passing by.

t_ch: 「赤红大哥」的熟人。
t_en: A close associate of Boss Red.

t_ch: …赤红大哥？好奇怪的名字。
t_en: ...Boss Red? What a strange name.

t_ch: 总之，多谢你的帮助，呃咳咳——
t_en: Anyway, thank you very much for your help. *cough*

t_ch: 那些家伙看不惯「隐夜鸫」不是一天两天了，但没想到他们会直接在这里动手。
t_en: The Nightingales have always been an eyesore to them, but I never thought they'd actually try anything here.

t_ch: 费歇尔被叫去朝露公馆，估计也是凶多吉少……
t_en: Fischer being summoned to Dewlight Pavilion probably also spells misfortune...

t_ch: 目前在这片梦域工作的筑梦师只有我和奥尔拉。不过奥尔拉还小，不能让他陷入危险中…我要把这些迷因都引开，能搭把手吗？
t_en: Orla and I are the only Dreamweavers in this dream domain. But Orla's still young, and shouldn't be confronted with such danger... I will distract those memes, will you lend a hand?

t_ch: 没问题。
t_en: No problem.

t_ch: 我就是为此而来。
t_en: That's what I'm here for.

t_ch: 谢谢你，不知名的好心人。我们得加快脚步了。
t_en: Thank you, kind stranger. We must hurry.

t_ch: （巴罗想要保护奥尔拉，但…他的身体似乎快撑不住了。）
t_en: (Barlow wants to protect Orla, but... he's not going to last.)

t_ch: 男人在尽力抵抗伤口带来的疼痛，但他的脚步无法控制地越来越慢。
t_en: The man is trying his best to withstand the pain from the wound, but his pace is obviously slowing down.

t_ch: 需要帮忙吗？
t_en: Do you need help?

t_ch: 我没事，是男人就得在关键时刻顶上来。
t_en: I'm fine, we have to step up at crucial moments.

t_ch: 奥尔拉…真希望他没事。
t_en: Orla... I hope he's all right.

t_ch: 听到你有危险就跑来了。
t_en: I ran over as soon as I heard you were in danger.

t_ch: 哈哈，他还是那么急性子……
t_en: Haha, he's always been the rash sort...

t_ch: 我也没找到他。
t_en: I couldn't find him either.

t_ch: 奥尔拉是我们中最小的孩子，也是最机灵的孩子。他做的「庇护所」越来越好，只要他不出来，就没人能破解……
t_en: Orla is the youngest among us, but the sharpest. The shelter he's crafted gets better with time. So long as he remains hidden, no one can break his creations...

t_ch: 但他还只是个孩子，本不该经受这些的。
t_en: But he's still just a child, and should not be subjected to this mess.

t_ch: 再走快点吧，我们得把追兵引开才行。
t_en: Let's hurry up, we have to lead our pursuers away.

t_ch: 可恶，猎犬的鼻子怎么这么好使……
t_en: Darn, those Hound noses sure are troublesome...

t_ch: 准备好，又要迎战了！
t_en: Get ready for another fight!

t_ch: 咳咳——迷因的数量还在增多，他们的增援马上就会过来……
t_en: *cough* The memes' numbers are increasing. Their reinforcements should be here any minute...

t_ch: 咱们快走吧！
t_en: We have to run!

t_ch: 哈…抱歉，我可能…就到此为止了。
t_en: Ha... Sorry, I might... have to stop here.

t_ch: 你的视线下移——不知何时，男人的身上已然遍布伤口。他早已无法继续挪动双腿了。
t_en: You look down — at some point, the man's body got covered in wounds. He can no longer move his legs.

t_ch: 我留下来当诱饵，你先走吧。
t_en: I'll stay behind and keep them busy, run.

t_ch: 奥尔拉修的藏身点应该就在附近，我知道过去的捷径。没必要两个人都交代在这。
t_en: Orla's hiding spot should be nearby, I know the shortcut to get there. No reason to sacrifice two lives here.

t_ch: 如果之后还会见到奥尔拉…替我说声抱歉吧。没法遵守约定，继续陪他锻炼了。
t_en: If you get a chance to see Orla... tell him I'm sorry I couldn't keep the promise to keep training with him.

t_ch: …我会帮你传达。
t_en: ...I will deliver the message.

t_ch: 那小孩儿身边的家人越来越少了，我们没法再护他周全…如果可以，以后也麻烦你多帮帮他。
t_en: The child's family is being whittled down. We can't keep him safe anymore... If you could, please help him out whenever possible in the future.

t_ch: …我不会帮你传话。
t_en: ...I won't deliver the message for you.

t_ch: 哈哈，那就扯个什么「巴罗已经去浪迹天涯」的谎吧。虽然他可能不信…但有希望，总归是个好事儿。
t_en: Haha, then just make up some lie about how Barlow has already gone off-world. Although he might not believe in it... But as long as there's hope, it should be a good thing.

t_ch: 谢谢你，以及——再见了，不知名的好心人。
t_en: Thank you, and — goodbye, nameless well-wisher.

t_ch: 打扰一下，{F#小姐}{M#先生}——请问您认识建造这里的筑梦师吗？
t_en: Excuse me, {F#miss}{M#sir} — do you know the Dreamweaver that built this place?

t_ch: 认识是认识…
t_en: I do...

t_ch: 但你是哪位？
t_en: But you are?

t_ch: 我名为费歇尔，是「隐夜鸫」的现任家主，此行来寻找本家系的成员奥尔拉。听说他最近在这附近活动，或许建造此处的筑梦师会知晓他的行踪。
t_en: My name is Fischer, I'm the current head of the Nightingale Family. I'm looking for a family member named Orla. I've heard he's been about this area lately, perhaps the Dreamweaver who built this place may know his whereabouts.

t_ch: 奥尔拉就是这里的创造者。
t_en: Orla is the creator of this place.

t_ch: 这可真是…令人惊叹。
t_en: This is... shocking.

t_ch: 当年那个到处捣乱的毛头小子，竟然已经能建出这种规模的建筑了……
t_en: The kid that used to be such a troublemaker has already built something of such scale...

t_ch: 话题扯远了。看来奥尔拉确实在这附近，我想与他见一面，能麻烦您带路吗？
t_en: I digress. It seems like Orla is indeed nearby, I wish to meet with him, could I trouble you to show me the way?

t_ch: …奥尔拉？
t_en: ...Orla?

t_ch: 蓝色的折纸鸟如玩偶般安静，它默不作声。
t_en: The blue origami bird is as quiet as a doll, silent.

t_ch: 这是…克劳克影城的周边玩偶？你果然不想见我。
t_en: This is... a Clock Studios Theme Park merchandise doll? You really don't want to see me.

t_ch: 算了，玩偶也行，如果你在玩偶后面的话…请不要走，听我把这句话说完吧。
t_en: Never mind, a doll is fine. If you're behind the doll... Please stay and hear me out.

t_ch: 谈判结束了，此后他们不会再牵连其他的筑梦师，也不会追究过去的恩怨。
t_en: The negotiations are over, they won't implicate other Dreamweavers nor will they be pursuing old grievances.

t_ch: 混乱已经结束，走吧，跟我回去吧。
t_en: The chaos is over. Come on, let's go back with me.

t_ch: 家族的人不会这么轻易地让步，你给了他们什么？
t_en: The Family doesn't budge so easily, what did you offer them?

t_ch: …「隐夜鸫」。他们想要整个「隐夜鸫」，我同意了。
t_en: ...Nightingale. They want the entire Nightingale, and I agreed.

t_ch: 此后，筑梦师会并入「家族」的管理下——但我们保下了折纸大学。以后所有的筑梦师都可以在那里学习，我们也可以更好地保护我们的成员！我——
t_en: From now on, the Dreamweavers will be managed by The Family — but we've kept the Paperfold University. In the future, all the Dreamweavers will be able to study there, and we will be able to protect our members better! I...

t_ch: 我不理解。你让格拉克斯教授的努力和大家的抗争都变得毫无意义。
t_en: I don't understand. You made Professor Glaux's efforts and everyone's defiance totally meaningless.

t_ch: 孩子，不是所有人都像巴罗他们那样勇敢，我们也没有资格逼迫所有人都拿出这份勇气。
t_en: My child, not everyone is as brave as Barlow and the rest. We have no right to force everyone to muster that kind of courage.

t_ch: 是，我们理解隐夜鸫的初心，我们可以牺牲，但其他隐夜鸫呢？他们大多数只是热爱着筑梦的普通人，无论是否归属家族，他们的梦想都不会被磨灭。
t_en: Yes, we understand the intent of the Nightingales. We can sacrifice ourselves, but what about the other Nightingales? Most of them are just normal people who love weaving dreams. Whether or not they belong to The Family, their dreams will not be erased.

t_ch: 不能让全部人都为我们的一腔私情买单，我必须思考能让大多数人幸福的办法，因为我是「家主」——这是格拉克斯教授交给我这份职责前的告诫。
t_en: We can't make everyone pay the price for our selfishness. I need to think of a way to make the majority happy because I am the family head. This is what Professor Glaux instructed before handing me this responsibility.

t_ch: 每一位筑梦师都很宝贵，我们已经不能再承受任何损失了。
t_en: Every single Dreamweaver is precious. We cannot afford any more losses.

t_ch: …懦夫。
t_en: ...Coward.

t_ch: 你说的对。但只有这样，隐夜鸫才不会消失，我们筑造的「巢」会永远留在梦中。
t_en: You're right. But this is the only way for the Nightingales not to disappear, and the "nest" we built will remain in the dreams forever.

t_ch: 抱歉，这已经是我能找到的最好的结局了。
t_en: I'm sorry, this is the best ending I could come up with.

t_ch: 折纸鸟不再说话，仿佛重新变回了玩偶。
t_en: The origami bird stopped speaking, as if it were a doll again.

t_ch: 乔伊曾说，筑梦是一首写在梦中的诗，将这处荒芜之地变为一片美梦，变为能为所有人带来幸福的家……
t_en: Joey once said that weaving dreams is like writing a poem within a dream, turning this desolate wasteland into Dreamjoy, into a home that brings happiness to all...

t_ch: 我会继续这份意志。现在还看不到什么成果，但或许有一天，筑梦之诗开花结果……
t_en: I will continue this will. There aren't any fruits of labor yet, but one day, the poem of dreamweaving will come to fruition...

t_ch: 那时，希望你能愿意再次回来。
t_en: By then, I hope you'll be willing to come back.

t_ch: 奥尔拉不愿再和我交流了…能帮我劝劝他吗？
t_en: Orla doesn't wish to speak to me anymore... Can you help me convince him?

t_ch: 请帮我把这张照片转交给他——只要他愿意回来，隐夜鸫永远都会是他的家。
t_en: Please pass this photo to him — as long as he's willing to return, the Nightingales will always be his home.

t_ch: 这是一张令人眼熟的照片——它曾打开过「湛蓝爵士」的内心，或许也能在此撬开「奥尔拉」的心房。
t_en: It's a familiar photo — it once opened Dr. Blues' heart, maybe it can open Orla's too.

t_ch: 蓝色的折纸鸟如玩偶般安静，它拒绝再次开口。
t_en: The blue origami bird is as quiet as a doll, it refuses to speak again.

t_ch: 费歇尔让我转交这张照片。
t_en: Fischer asked me to pass you this photo.

t_ch: 小鸟的羽毛抖了抖，最终还是没有接过照片。
t_en: The bird's feathers ruffle slightly, but ultimately, it does not accept the photo.

t_ch: …我都想起来了。
t_en: ...I remember now.

t_ch: 筑梦师失去了他的最后的家人，于是在匹诺康尼的最下方建了一处庇护所，就此躲在里面。
t_en: The Dreamweaver lost the last of his family, so he built a shelter at the bottom of Penacony and is hiding there.

t_ch: 他躲在梦境之底，却时刻在仰视着上方的天空——他唾弃费歇尔的宣言，却仍期待着费歇尔口中「开花结果」的某日。
t_en: He hides at the bottom of the Dreamscape, yet his gaze is fixed upon the heavens above — he scorns Fischer's proclamations, yet he yearns for the day when Fischer's words come to fruition.

t_ch: 时间又过去了很久…他的身体先等不下去了，于是爱撒谎的家犬为他虚构了新的身体，那是「钟表匠」为筑梦师画下的肖像——一只天蓝色的折纸小鸟。
t_en: A long time passed... His body was unable to withstand the test of time, so that compulsive liar of a Hound made a new body for him — a sky-blue origami bird that the Watchmaker painted as a portrait for the Dreamweaver.

t_ch: 那就是「湛蓝爵士」…
t_en: That is Dr. Blues...

t_ch: 也是曾经的奥尔拉…
t_en: Formerly, Orla...

t_ch: 但看来等的时间太长，他的心灵与记忆也有等不下去的一天……
t_en: However, the prolonged wait has taken its toll. His mind and memories can no longer endure the wait...

t_ch: 呵，费歇尔就是个说大话的骗子。他再也没有回来过，其他人也没回来过。「钟表匠」和加拉赫，他们也都等到了自己的「完美结局」。
t_en: Ah, Fischer is just a huge liar. He has never been back, and neither has anyone else. The Watchmaker and Gallagher both waited for their own "perfect ending."

t_ch: 看看现在的匹诺康尼，繁华、美丽、灯火通明，就像乔伊跟我许诺的一样。
t_en: Look at Penacony now, bustling, beautiful, bright, exactly what Joey promised me.

t_ch: 而不认可这份美好的人们，我也为他们留下了流梦礁，没人会在梦中流浪……
t_en: For those who don't agree with this beauty, I offer the Dreamflux Reef as their sanctuary — no one will ever feel deserted in dreams.

t_ch: 折纸筑梦师们已经完成了他们的承诺，但…我不能接受没有他们的梦境。
t_en: The Origami Dreamweavers have fulfilled their promise, but... I cannot accept a Dreamscape without them.

t_ch: 我已经没有坚持的理由了，也该迎接我并不完美的结局了——
t_en: There's no reason for me to hold on, it's time for me to embrace my imperfect end...

t_ch: 你还有未来。
t_en: You still have a future.

t_ch: 呵呵，你是说让我当一只卡通小鸟，不能筑梦、不能认识朋友、甚至不能让别人看见，这样继续活在梦里吗？
t_en: Hehe, are you telling me to merely exist as a cartoon bird, devoid of the ability to weave dreams, to make friends, and to remain invisible to the eyes of others? Is this the solitary existence I am to endure within the realm of dreams?

t_ch: 无名客，我没有未来，我是「过去」。
t_en: Nameless, I have no future, I am the past.

t_ch: 你还有其他的折纸小鸟。
t_en: You still have the other origami birds.

t_ch: 那些小鸟只是「钟表匠」无心促成的造物吧…那家伙，总是喜欢做这种奇怪的事情。
t_en: Those birds are just the unintentional creations of the Watchmaker... That guy's always doing weird stuff like this.

t_ch: 但我理解他——匹诺康尼在忘记过去，但哪怕只有一点也好，这个世界应该留下往日的痕迹。我怕哪天…我也会忘记那些时光。
t_en: But I understand him — Penacony has forgotten its past. But even if just a little, this world ought to preserve a fragment of its past. I fear for the day... when I, too, shall begin to forget those cherished moments...

t_ch: …说到底，我始终是那个最胆小、最幼稚的家伙。大家都有想要建造的梦想，而我——只是想要一个鸟儿的「巢」吧。
t_en: ...In the end, I'm still the most timid and naive one. Everyone has a dream to build, but I... only want a nest for the birds.

t_ch: 如果说匹诺康尼是一片梦想之地…那么，只有一次也好，我希望这片梦想之地回应一次我的愿望——让我回到那个鸟巢吧。
t_en: If Penacony is a land of dreams... Then, even if it's just once, I yearn for this land of dreams to heed my plea — let me return to the nest.

t_ch: ——奥尔拉？
t_en: ...Orla?

t_ch: 还呆着做什么，等你很久了。
t_en: What are you doing here? I've been waiting for you.

t_ch: 哼哼，吓傻了？快来试试我新做的「冯恩四世」，全面改进升级——就差你来测试了！
t_en: Heh-heh, are you shocked? Come try my new Vaughn IV, fully improved and upgraded — you're the only one who hasn't tried it!

t_ch: 你们…来接我了？
t_en: You guys... are here to pick me up?

t_ch: 呵呵，我们不是约好了在上面见吗？太久等不到人，就决定直接下来咯。
t_en: Haha, didn't we agree to meet up there? I waited so long and didn't see anyone, so I decided to come straight down.

t_ch: 我这次不会离开了，在这片梦境中，我们再也不会分开。
t_en: I'm not leaving again, and in this Dreamscape, we shall never be separated again.

t_ch: 嗯…朋友们，我这就来！
t_en: Yes... my friends, here I come!

t_ch: 小鸟扇动翅膀，它的身体逐渐变得透明，他的模因身体正在逐渐飘散。
t_en: The bird flaps its wings, its body slowly becoming transparent, its memetic body gradually drifting away.

t_ch: （奥尔拉的意志减弱了…再这样下去，他<color=#dbc291ff>将被自己的梦境吞噬</color>！）
t_en: (Orla's will is fading... If this continues, he will be <color=#dbc291ff>consumed by his own Dreamscape</color>!)

t_ch: （唤醒奥尔拉）
t_en: (Wake Orla)

t_ch: （放任奥尔拉离开）
t_en: (Let Orla leave)

t_ch: 回忆对小鸟虚构的身体来说太过沉重，他希望你就此放手。
t_en: Memories are too heavy for a birdie's illusory body. He hopes you can let him go.

t_ch: 小鸟的身体正被梦境吞噬。如果想把奥尔拉带回去…这就是最后的机会了。
t_en: The bird's body is being devoured by the Dreamscape. If you want to bring Orla back... this would be your last chance.

t_ch: （必须唤醒奥尔拉！）
t_en: (I must wake Orla!)

t_ch: （放任奥尔拉离开）
t_en: (Let Orla leave)

t_ch: 你没有阻止。小鸟轻盈地朝着光飞去，飞向其他小鸟的身边。在光芒中，你恍然看到鸟变为一位少年，他转头向你轻轻微笑。
t_en: You didn't intervene. The bird flies gracefully toward the light, joining the other birds in flight. Within the radiance, you witness the bird's transformation into a young man, who turns his head and offers you a gentle smile.

t_ch: 谢谢你，无名客。
t_en: Thank you, Nameless.

t_ch: 我终于到家啦。
t_en: I'm finally home.

t_ch: 不要被梦境操控！
t_en: Don't be controlled by the Dreamscape!

t_ch: 那只是过去的影子…
t_en: That's just a shadow of the past...

t_ch: …嗯，我知道。
t_en: ...Yes, I know.

t_ch: 你明知道…？
t_en: You knew...?

t_ch: 思想和情绪可以在忆域中化作具象，这是最基础的筑梦知识。
t_en: Thoughts and emotions can materialize in the Memory Zone — That's the foundation of dreamweaving.

t_ch: 但哪怕我知道这是梦，也不愿选择醒来——
t_en: Even though I know this is just a dream, I don't want to wake up...

t_ch: 我太久没见过他们的样子了。梦中人无法再次入梦，我连他们的脸都要忘了…就此深陷于梦中，也许就是属于我的完美结局。
t_en: I haven't seen their faces for so long. Dreamers are unable to enter dreams again. I've almost forgotten their faces... Perhaps being trapped deep in a dream is the perfect ending for me.

t_ch: 曾经的几位传奇筑梦师站在远处，为奥尔拉指出通往美梦的方向。
t_en: A few legendary Dreamweavers of the past stand in the distance, guiding Orla toward Dreamjoy.

t_ch: 那你更要醒来。
t_en: That's all the more reason for you to wake up.

t_ch: （放任他离开）
t_en: (Let him go)

t_ch: 小鸟的身影越发透明，它向着伙伴、向着自己的美梦奔去。
t_en: The bird's silhouette grows increasingly translucent as it runs towards its companions and the embrace of its own yearning.

t_ch: …但对它来说，这真的是<color=#dbc291ff>「最好的结局」</color>吗？
t_en: ...But to it, is this really the <color=#dbc291ff>best ending</color>?

t_ch: （必须唤醒奥尔拉！）
t_en: (I must wake Orla!)

t_ch: （仍放任奥尔拉离开）
t_en: (Let Orla go)

t_ch: 你没有阻止。小鸟轻盈地朝着光飞去，飞向其他小鸟的身边。在光芒中，你恍然看到鸟变为一位少年，他转头向你轻轻微笑。
t_en: You didn't intervene. The bird flies gracefully toward the light, joining the other birds in flight. Within the radiance, you witness the bird's transformation into a young man, who turns his head and offers you a gentle smile.

t_ch: 谢谢你，无名客。
t_en: Thank you, Nameless.

t_ch: 我终于到家啦。
t_en: I'm finally home.

t_ch: 正因珍视——才不能用虚伪之物替代。
t_en: Because it's precious — It should not be replaced by something false.

t_ch: 你需要把他们的故事带回去，重新讲给匹诺康尼，让所有人知道——究竟是谁筑起了这片美梦。
t_en: You need to bring their stories back, recount these stories to Penacony, and let everyone know exactly who built this Dreamjoy.

t_ch: 我知道你已经记下了他们的故事。
t_en: I know you've already recorded their stories.

t_ch: 时间没能留下的真相，由你带回去就足够了。
t_en: It's enough to have you bring back the truth that time did not manage to preserve.

t_ch: 但还缺一个监督者。
t_en: But there still needs to be an overseer.

t_ch: 为了防止他们的故事再被篡改，这里需要一位监督者。
t_en: An overseer is required to prevent their story from being altered yet again.

t_ch: 我想…一位见证过历史的筑梦师就是最好的选择。
t_en: I think... a Dreamweaver that has witnessed history would be the best choice.

t_ch: 你不在我可就要乱讲了。
t_en: If you're not there, then I'm gonna start spewing nonsense.

t_ch: 你可不要怪我到处乱讲——筑梦师奥尔拉因为害怕挨揍而哭鼻子！还不止一次！
t_en: Don't blame me for spreading nonsense — Dreamweaver Orla cries because he's afraid of getting punched! And he did it more than once!

t_ch: 你！
t_en: You!

t_ch: …油嘴滑舌的无名客，和「钟表匠」真是一模一样。
t_en: ...Smooth-talking Nameless. You're exactly like the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 还要我继续说吗？
t_en: Do you want me to keep going?

t_ch: …算了，就当是你赢了吧。
t_en: ...Fine, you win.

t_ch: 既然费歇尔完成了他的承诺，那按照约定，我也该…偶尔回去看看。
t_en: Since Fischer upheld his promise, then as agreed, I should... go back to see how things are once in a while.

t_ch: 梦境的主人做出了选择。它最后看了一眼过去的同伴，转身走向你。
t_en: The Dreamscape's owner has made his choice. It takes one last look at his past companions, and turns to move towards you.

t_ch: 在这儿待得也够久了。走吧，该走了——
t_en: I've stayed here long enough. Come on. It's time to go...

t_ch: …这次，我不会再忘记了。
t_en: ...This time, I won't forget.

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: （还是保持沉默）
t_en: (Continue remaining silent)

t_ch: （继续保持沉默！）
t_en: (Continue to remain silent!)
...Hey.

t_ch: 奥尔拉？
t_en: Orla?

t_ch: 奥尔拉？
t_en: Orla?

t_ch: 奥尔拉？
t_en: Orla?

t_ch: …听见了听见了。这个名字从你嘴里吐出来总感觉怪怪的…你还是叫我「湛蓝爵士」吧。
t_en: ...I heard you the first time. This name feels weird coming out of your mouth... Call me Dr. Blues.

t_ch: 总之——真相也找了，梦也打碎了，现在满意了吗？
t_en: Anyways — The truth was found, and the dream was broken. Happy now?

t_ch: 满意了。
t_en: Happy.

t_ch: 感觉还差口气…
t_en: Feels like it needs something more...

t_ch: 谢谢，别再来第二次了。老年鸟受不了这个。
t_en: Thank you, and don't do it again. This old bird can't take it.

t_ch: 助人为乐，不客气。
t_en: I'm happy to help. You're welcome.

t_ch: 没在夸你！哎…这点怎么也跟「钟表匠」一样。你们无名客都是一个模子里出来的吗？
t_en: I wasn't complimenting you! *sigh* ...Why are you exactly like the Watchmaker? Are all you Nameless cut from the same cloth?

t_ch: 算了，就先这样吧。
t_en: Forget it. Let's leave it be.

t_ch: 小鸟抬头望向星空——十二片梦境的灯火连成一片，组成了流梦礁的星空。
t_en: The bird looks up at the starry sky — The lights from the twelve Dreamscapes link up to form Dreamflux Reef's starry sky.

t_ch: …费歇尔果然没骗我，他真的将这里变成了一首诗。
t_en: ...Fischer didn't lie to me. He really turned this place into a poem.

t_ch: 要上去看看吗？
t_en: Do you want to go up and take a look?

t_ch: 下次吧。托某位无名客的福，本鸟刚做了一场大梦，得先缓缓。
t_en: Maybe next time. Thanks to a certain Nameless, I've just had a huge dream. I'll have to take a break.

t_ch: …不管怎么说，能再见他们一次，还是挺好的。
t_en: ...No matter what, it was great to be able to see them again.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 不客气。
t_en: You're welcome.

t_ch: 听不见，大点声！
t_en: I can't hear you. Say it louder!

t_ch: ——喂，别蹬鼻子上脸！
t_en: ...Hey, don't push your luck!

t_ch: ——啾，啾啾！找到啦！
t_en: — Tweet, tweet tweet! Found it!

t_en: A familiar tweeting sound comes from the starry sky above — four chubby birds descend from the sky and land beside Dr. Blues.

t_ch: 不听话的湛蓝弟弟，终于找到了啾～
t_en: Naughty little Blues, we finally found you! Tweet!

t_ch: 哼哼，等不到你，于是本公子只好屈尊来下面的「大树」了…不必谢我啾～

t_en: Tweet tweet, so the sky seen from below is beautiful too.

t_en: This way, Dr. Blues can always see our faces, tweet!

t_ch: 好心的客人，能帮我们一起拍张合照吗，啾啾？
t_en: Kind guest, could you help us take a group photo, tweet tweet?

t_en: No problem!

t_en: Leave it to me.

t_en: (Press the shutter)

t_ch: 你们、你们怎么突然过来了啾——

t_ch: 嘿呀——不见的日子，有在认真锻炼吗？
t_en: Hey — Did you keep up with your training since our last encounter?

t_ch: 啾啾，原来从下往上看的天空也很漂亮呢～
t_en: Tweet tweet, so the sky seen from below is beautiful too.

t_en: The four origami birds surround the blue bird, tweeting in unison. You see Dr. Blues' sincere smile.

t_en: You alone hold the memory of the Dreamweavers' arduous and painful past.

t_en: (Press the shutter)

t_en: Perhaps... to Dr. Blues, this is what family is. It really did find a home.

t_en: We meet again, {F#madam}{M#sir}.

t_en: I've come to return something.

t_en: You're back faster than I thought. Is the investigation over already?

t_ch: 是的，我已经调查完了。
t_en: Yes, I'm done with the investigation.

t_en: Nope, but we don't need them anymore.

t_en: Hehe, from your expression, I can tell this isn't going to be a light-hearted story.

t_en: A pity, my position limits the help I can offer.

t_en: But... my mentor Mr. Fischer once told me this. I hope it can bring you some sort of comfort.

t_en: "If you stumble when weaving dreams, look up."

t_ch: 「看向这片梦境，然后告诉自己：你的努力从不会白费。总有一日，它们将连成星光，照亮某人的眼睛。」
t_en: "Look upon this vast Dreamscape and tell yourself that your endeavors are never in vain. One day, they shall be woven together into a celestial tapestry, casting radiant light into the depths of another's eyes."

t_ch: 愿你能寻到自己的巢，愿你能书写自己的诗。
t_en: May you find your own nest, and write your own poem.

t_en: ...Tick-tock! Here, I'm over there!

t_ch: （去走廊找找他们口中的人吧。）
t_en: (Let's head to the corridor to look for that person they mentioned.)

t_ch: ——就在那！别让他跑了！！
t_en: ...Over there! Don't let him get away!

t_ch: 罪人，你的行为早已触犯家族的底线！
t_en: Sinner, your actions have long tested the limits of The Family!

t_ch: 放弃抵抗！否则我们就不客气了！
t_en: Do not resist! Or you'll be sorry!

t_en: (The Dreamweavers' golden age has ended here...)

t_ch: 就算是最挑剔的冯恩，应该也会忍不住惊叫吧……

t_ch: 真想看看他们的表情。
t_en: I really want to see their faces now.

t_ch: 前段时间…外面不算太平，不少筑梦师也被牵连。

t_ch: 奥尔拉与那些被牵连的筑梦师关系很好，也被卷进了这场风波。

t_ch: 好在他没事。

t_ch: 家系已经失去了很多人，不能再失去任何一个了……
t_en: The lineage's lost many people, and couldn't afford to lose even a single more...

t_ch: 无论如何，现在外面的情况已经稳定下来了。奥尔拉也不用在梦境的缝隙里躲躲藏藏，可以回家了。
t_en: In any case, the situation outside has calmed down. Orla doesn't have to hide in the crevices of the Dreamscape. He can go home now.

t_ch: 我来接他回家。
t_en: I'm here to take him home.

t_ch: （筑梦师聚集的地方…去筑梦边境问问看吧。）
t_en: (The place where Dreamweavers gather... Let's ask around bat the Dream's Edge.)

t_ch: （或许「家族」的筑梦师们知道些什么。）
t_en: (Maybe The Family's Dreamweavers know something.)

t_en: (So the origami bird's original form is a nightingale... Was Dr. Blues also once The Family's Dreamweaver?)

t_ch: （回流梦礁问问它吧。）
t_en: (Time to go back to Dreamflux Reef and ask it.)

t_en: I'd like to help you recover your memory.

t_en: Hey, Orla!

t_ch: 呵呵~走吧，该去找找那孩子藏起的钥匙拼图了。
t_en: Heh heh... Let's go then. We should look for the jigsaw key that the child hid.

t_ch: （「奥尔拉」…这大概就是湛蓝爵士真正的名字。他受到两位筑梦师的邀请，加入了隐夜鸫家系。）
t_en: (Orla... That's probably Dr. Blues' real name. He was invited by the two Dreamweavers to join the Nightingale Family.)

t_ch: （而乔伊与冯恩…这两位折纸筑梦师的言行也与档案记录中的样子有所出入。）
t_en: (And Joey and Vaughn... The personalities of these two Origami Dreamweavers are also quite different from what was described in the files.)

t_ch: （就如惠特克爵士所言，真正的历史总被时间磨损，或许我该将这些都记录下来……）
t_en: (It's just as Sir Whittaker said — that real history would always be ground down by time. Maybe I should record all these...)

t_ch: （咦？远方那只蓝色小鸟…是湛蓝爵士？）
t_en: (Huh? That blue bird in the distance... Is that Dr. Blues?)

t_ch: 桌上放着一张略有破损的《逐梦客时讯》，中间印着这样一条报道。
t_en: A mildly worn Dreamchaser Bulletin rests on the table, with a news headline printed in its middle.

t_ch: 「『白日梦酒店』竣工开业，第一届谐乐大典成功举办，各位家族成员见证『同谐』化身的降临……」
t_en: "The completed Reverie Hotel has opened. The inaugural Charmony Festival was successfully held, and all Family members witnessed the descent of the avatar of the Harmony..."

t_ch: 「十二时刻梦境建设进入尾声。橡木家系家主歌斐木表示：『五大家系联合一心』，隐夜鸫家系或将并入『家族』？」
t_en: "Construction of the Twelve Hours of the Dreamscapes is almost near its end. The Oak Family Head Gopher Wood declares the "unity of the five major lineages." Will the Nightingale join The Family?"

t_ch: （…这就是筑梦师巴罗的结局。）
t_en: (...This is Dreamweaver Barlow's end.)

t_ch: （巴罗并不是沽名钓誉的叛徒…他是为同伴奋斗到最后一刻的英雄。）
t_en: (Barlow was no vainglorious traitor... He was a hero who fought for his companions till the last moment.)

t_ch: （他的故事该被记录下来……）
t_en: (His story deserves to be recorded...)

t_ch: （不知这场抗争的结果如何。或许能在奥尔拉的记忆中找到最后的真相……）
t_en: (I wonder how this resistance ended. Maybe I can find the true ending in Orla's memories...)

t_ch: 费歇尔先生…哎，我知道此行的目的。但奥尔拉说他不愿意见你。
t_en: Mr. Fischer... Look, I know the reason behind this trip, but Orla says he doesn't want to see you.

t_ch: 他大概不会亲自露面。我已经将您带过来了，后续的沟通就得靠您自己了。
t_en: He's probably not going to show up in person. I've brought you here, but how this goes is up to you now.

t_ch: 以及…不管怎么说，{RUBY_B#流梦礁}这里{RUBY_E#}仍是一处隐秘之地，希望您不要向外透露这里的信息。
t_en: And... no matter what, {RUBY_B#Dreamflux Reef}this place{RUBY_E#} is still a concealed secret location. I hope you won't reveal it to outsiders.

t_ch: …那你呢？我此行是来带所有筑梦师回到隐夜鸫…你愿意跟我回去吗？
t_en: ...What about you? I came here to bring all the Dreamweavers back to the Nightingales... Will you come with me?

t_ch: 隐夜鸫，多么令人怀念的名字……
t_en: Nightingales. Such a nostalgic name...

t_ch: 但我的身体已经没法再接受更多的变故了，费歇尔先生。我老了，也该退休了。
t_en: But my body cannot withstand much more mishap, Mr. Fischer. I'm old, and I should be retired.

t_ch: …抱歉。
t_en: ...My apologies.

t_ch: 呵呵，没什么可抱歉的，这并不是你的错……
t_en: Heh-heh, there's nothing to be sorry for. It's not your fault...

t_ch: 去吧，一直向前走——你要找的人在那边等着你呢。
t_en: Go, keep forging ahead — the one you wish to find is waiting for you there.

t_ch: 路边躺着一叠破损的《逐梦客时讯》，几条不同年代的报道夹杂其中。
t_en: A stack of torn Dreamchaser Bulletins is lying by the roadside, with reports from different times mixed in it.

t_ch: 「隐夜鸫家系家主格拉克斯引退，五大家系合并或成定局？」
t_en: "Nightingale Family head Glaux retires. Will the unity of the five major lineages be inevitable?"

t_ch: 「『匹诺康尼折纸大学学院』正式建成。前隐夜鸫家主格拉克斯出任第一任校长，开设『筑梦学院』以为匹诺康尼培养更优秀的筑梦师人才。」
t_en: "Penacony Paperfold University has officially completed construction. Former Nightingale Family head Glaux becomes first dean, then opens Dreamweaver Academy to create more outstanding Dreamweavers."

t_ch: 「十二时刻梦境建设基本完成，漫长的『筑梦工程』计划圆满成功——笔者相信，这将是匹诺康尼最好的时代，伟大的『逐梦时代』……」
t_en: "Construction of the Twelve Hours in the Dreamscape is almost complete, and the prolonged Dreamweaver Project is a success. The author believes that this will be Penacony's finest age, the grand Age of Dreaming..."

t_ch: 蓝色的折纸鸟如玩偶般安静，它拒绝再次开口。
t_en: The blue origami bird is as quiet as a doll, it refuses to speak again.

t_ch: 又见面了，{F#小姐}{M#先生}。
t_en: We meet again, {F#madam}{M#sir}.

t_ch: 我来还档案。
t_en: I'm here to return the file.

t_ch: 你回来的速度比我想象中快许多，调查已经结束了吗？
t_en: You're back faster than I thought. Is the investigation over already?

t_ch: 是的，我已经调查完了。
t_en: Yes, I'm done with the investigation.

t_ch: 你向惠特克爵士转述了那个属于筑梦师的逐梦年代——以及那些文字并未记载的，最真实的他们。
t_en: You tell Sir Whittaker about the Dreamchaser era that belonged to the Dreamweavers — and the truest form of those people, which was unrecorded in history.

t_ch: 原来如此…上任家主费歇尔先生并未向我转述这段往事。
t_en: I see... Mr. Fischer, the former Family head, did not tell me about this past.

t_ch: 感谢你的这份记录，我会妥善保存的。
t_en: I thank you for this record. I will keep it secure.

t_ch: 你会公布这份记录吗？
t_en: Will you publish this record?

t_ch: 我很想答应您——但很遗憾，我的立场要求我的行为必须谨慎。那些影响家系稳定的部分或许无法公开，但我保证，梦境会记住这些筑梦师的名字。
t_en: I'd really like to say yes, but alas, my political stance requires that I be prudent about my actions. I may not be able to publicize the parts that affect The Family's stability, but I promise that the Dreamscape will remember the names of those Dreamweavers.

t_ch: 大概要等很久了…
t_en: I guess it will be a long wait...

t_ch: 很可惜，即使身处梦境，匹诺康尼也无法满足所有人的心愿。
t_en: A pity that, even in the Dreamscape, Penacony is unable to fulfill everyone's wishes.

t_ch: 不过…我的恩师费歇尔先生曾赠给我一句话，希望它也能为你提供几分宽慰。
t_en: But... my mentor Mr. Fischer once told me this. I hope it can bring you some sort of comfort.

t_ch: ——「若是筑梦时被绊住腿脚，就抬头吧。」
t_en: "If you stumble when weaving dreams, look up."

t_ch: 「看向这片梦境，然后告诉自己：你的努力从不会白费。总有一日，它们将连成星光，照亮某人的眼睛。」
t_en: "Look upon this vast Dreamscape and tell yourself that your endeavors are never in vain. One day, they shall be woven together into a celestial tapestry, casting radiant light into the depths of another's eyes."

t_ch: 愿你能寻到自己的巢，愿你能书写自己的诗。
t_en: May you find your own nest, and write your own poem.

t_ch: 各位公民，你们也都看到了，就是这个外邦人！
t_en: Citizens, we have seen it too, it was this outlander!

t_ch: 我心爱的大壮之所以会性情大变、连着几天不愿意接客，全是因为这个外邦人做了手脚。
t_en: That my beloved Bossman would exhibit such major mood swings, refusing to receive guests for days on end, it is the trickery of this outlander.

t_ch: 胡说！我、我只是轻轻摸了一把…谁知道你们这儿的大地兽胆子这么小啊。
t_en: Slander! I—I only petted it lightly... Who knew your dromas would be so timid?

t_ch: 哼，「轻轻」摸了一把？我手下这些大地兽可都是被{RUBY_B#大地之泰坦}吉奥里亚{RUBY_E#}祝福过的圣兽，十分娇贵！
t_en: Hmph, "lightly"? My dromases are sacred beasts blessed by {RUBY_B#Earth Titan}Georios{RUBY_E#}. They are nigh priceless!

t_ch: 就拿这大壮来说，它一旦受到惊吓就吃不进饭；吃不进饭，就没力气驮人；它不驮人，我一天就要少赚许多钱！
t_en: Like Bossman here, it loses its appetite when it's frightened. Without food, it won't have strength to carry people, and if it doesn't work, I'll lose that much more money in a day!

t_ch: 再看这漂亮的鳞片，脏是脏了点，但…我去，上面什么时候有的划痕？肯定是刚才被你摸坏了！
t_en: And look at these beautiful scales, they are a little shabby, but... Wuh, when did this scratch appear? You must have done this with your grubby fingers!

t_ch: 啊？这…这是它原本就有的啊。
t_en: Eh? That... That was already there to begin with.

t_ch: 别在那儿狡辩了。除了你，今天还有谁碰过它？你必须赔偿我的损失！
t_en: Stop making excuses, no one else touched it besides you that day. You will pay for my losses!

t_ch: 发生甚么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 哦，瞧瞧谁来了？连跟黄金裔并肩作战的英雄都站在我这边——果然公道自在人心。
t_en: Oh, look who's here? Even the hero who fought alongside the Chrysos Heirs stands with me — justice does lie in the hearts of people.

t_ch: 外邦人？
t_en: Outlander?

t_ch: 没错，来自圣城之外、觊觎我们财富的家伙——可不能对他们手软。
t_en: That's right, for these scoundrels from outside the holy city who covet our wealth, their punishment must be swift and just.

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 后到场的朋友们别担心吃不着瓜，我这就把来龙去脉再讲一遍……
t_en: Those who have just arrived, worry not, for I shall reiterate the story once more...

t_ch: 最近这段时间，大壮不知为啥总是犯懒。我看事出反常，就亲自到兽栏蹲守，果然发现了元凶——这个外邦人！
t_en: In recent times, Bossman had been unusually lazy. I noticed something amiss, and personally stood guard by its cage. Guess who I caught red-handed — this outlander!

t_ch: 他每天准时准点来到这里，跑到大壮旁边、在它身上猛掐。我的心都要滴血了！
t_en: He would come here on time every day, get close to Bossman and pinch it violently. My heart bleeds for its abuse!

t_ch: 那、那是按摩！我看它状态不对，估计平时没少挨累，就想帮它缓解一下……
t_en: I—I was massaging it! I noticed something off with its behavior, surmising that it was overworked, and wanted to ease its pain...

t_ch: 你这老板都不让驮兽歇口气，连续工作一年不休息，就是山之民也吃不消啊。
t_en: An owner like you — working your beasts of burden to the bone without a day off — even mountain dwellers would be hard-pressed to keep up.

t_ch: 我…我们奥赫玛自己的驯养方针，什么时候轮到你个外邦人指手画脚了？难道你不尊重我们的习俗？
t_en: H—How we rear our livestock in Okhema will not be dictated by an ignorant outlander such as you! Are you disrespecting our customs?

t_ch: 确实，虽然我不懂大地兽，但这个外邦人未免太不守规矩了……
t_en: That's right, I don't know much about dromases, but this outlander is overstepping his boundaries...

t_ch: 就是，说不定又是{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}的遗民，手脚真不干净……
t_en: Yeah, that one might even be another remnant of {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#}'s worshipers. Look at them sneaking about...

t_ch: 听到了吗？按照律法，现在你只有两个选择。要么赔钱私了，要么咱们公民法庭上见。
t_en: Hear that? According to our laws, you now have two choices: Resolve this with compensation, or I'll see you in civilian court.

t_ch: 啊？可我没钱啊……
t_en: Hah? But I have no money...

t_ch: 那就怪不得我了——卫兵呢？过来干活了！
t_en: Don't blame me then... Where are the guards? It's time to earn your keep!

t_ch: 你这未免太霸道了些。
t_en: That's a little overboard, isn't it?

t_ch: 霸道？照你这么说，要求犯人赔偿也是霸道？
t_en: Overboard? I'm in the wrong for seeking compensation from a criminal?

t_ch: 拉莱德大人，此言差矣。虽然这位{F#姑娘}{M#青年}的言辞稍显朴素，但道理并没有错。
t_en: Lord Liluder, you are mistaken. This {F#lass'}{M#lad's} words may have been candid, but the reasoning is sound.

t_ch: 全场消费由我开拓者买单。
t_en: As a Trailblazer, I'll take care of all expenses.

t_ch: …你是想自己替他赔钱？可那样还怎么给他个教训？
t_en: ...You wish to pay the compensation for him? But how will that teach him a lesson?

t_ch: 拉莱德大人，这位{F#姑娘}{M#青年}是出于善意才提此建议，你若真接受，只怕是内心过于强大了。
t_en: Lord Liluder, the suggestion from this {F#lass}{M#lad} came from a place of goodwill. If you really do accept it, being overly compassionate would be your only fault.

t_ch: 听见了没有，赔钱！
t_en: You heard that? Pay up!

t_ch: 怎么总感觉你喊得比我还大声…算了。
t_en: Why does it sound like you're just trying to raise your voice over mine... Forget it.

t_ch: 那是因为站在你身后的并非只有支持者，拉莱德大人，他们也可能是为了看你出糗。
t_en: That is because those standing behind you aren't just mere supporters, Lord Liluder, they may be anticipating your fall from grace.

t_ch: 打起来，打起来~
t_en: Fight, fight, fight!

t_ch: …这就不必了吧。我只是想要钱而已，教训外邦人这活还轮不到我亲自做。
t_en: ...That won't be necessary. I am only interested in money. It's not up to me to personally mete out punishment on outlanders.

t_ch: 拉莱德大人，这位{F#姑娘}{M#青年}的言外之意是，你的咄咄逼人已经把局面带到了冲突的边缘。
t_en: Lord Liluder, what this {F#lass}{M#lad} means is that, your overbearing antics have escalated this conflict to the brink of physical violence.

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 拉莱德大人，这样的做法恐怕有些太不近人情吧？
t_en: Lord Liluder, such methods are a tad unreasonable, no?

t_ch: 在矛盾逐渐激化时，一个老者从人群中走了出来。
t_en: As the conflict gradually reaches breaking point, a geezer emerges from the crowd.

t_ch: 你又是哪根葱？
t_en: And who're you?

t_ch: 只是一个普通的老者。拉莱德大人，我看你气势十足，想必已是心怀事理。关于这些驮兽，在下有些问题想请教。
t_en: Just an ordinary geriatric. Lord Liluder, I reckon you must have knowledge of the matter, from your confident poise. I have a few questions regarding these pack beasts.

t_ch: 哼，不光大地兽，我对经手的各种商品都了如指掌。你问吧。
t_en: Hmph, not only the dromases, I also know my merchandise like the back of my hand. Ask away.

t_ch: 作为大地兽的庇护主，{RUBY_B#大地之泰坦}吉奥里亚{RUBY_E#}最为人知的特性是什么？
t_en: As the guardian of these dromases, what is the most commonly known characteristic of {RUBY_B#Earth Titan}Georios{RUBY_E#}?

t_ch: 那还用说？当然是吃苦耐劳、坚韧不拔、皮糙肉厚！
t_en: Need you even ask? It's earnest hardship, tenacity and a thick hide!

t_ch: 那么，被吉奥里亚祝福过的大地兽，又该是什么表现呢？
t_en: Then, how should dromases, blessed by Georios, be manifested?

t_ch: 当然是一样………
t_en: In the same way, obviously...

t_ch: 听到这里，各位或许心有疑惑——倘若眼前这头大地兽真被泰坦祝福过，如此坚韧的生物，仅凭赤手又怎能在它的鳞片上留下划痕呢？
t_en: On this note, everyone may be asking a question: If this dromas here was blessed by a Titan, how could such a tough creature have a scratch on its scales?

t_ch: 对此我有一个猜测：先前城邦遇袭时，疯王眷属在城内四处破坏，不少大地兽也受到了波及。
t_en: I shall hazard a guess at this: When the city was attacked, the Mad King's Kin wrought destruction along the streets, causing damage to many dromases.

t_ch: 它鳞片上的伤痕是荣耀的证明。只是因为亲近大地，体表的泥土遮住了伤痕，所以始终没人发觉。
t_en: The scars on its scales are a testament to its glory. But as a servant of the earth, mud covered its scar, preventing anyone from noticing.

t_ch: …直到那位先生为它按摩，鳞片表面的泥土被抖落，伤痕才随之显现。
t_en: ...Till that gentleman gave it a massage, causing the mud on its scales to be kneaded off, and thus was the scar revealed.

t_ch: 哈！真是能说会道。但你以为有了这番说辞，他就不用赔钱了吗？
t_en: Ha! How ludicrous. You think your outrageous rhetoric will acquit him of having to compensate me?

t_ch: 如果你不喜欢，倒也存在其他解释：比如这头大地兽并没有受到祝福，只是出奇脆弱而已；又或者…划痕根本就不是那位先生弄的。
t_en: If you didn't like that, there are other theories as well: That this dromas wasn't blessed at all, and is just unusually frail. Or... the scar wasn't even made by that gentleman to begin with.

t_ch: 真相究竟是什么样，相信拉莱德大人心知肚明。究竟要采用哪种解释，选择权在你。
t_en: I believe Lord Liluder is most aware of the truth. You are free to choose your explanation, no matter which one it may be.

t_ch: 你…算了，今天的事就先不追究了。但千万别再让我见到那个外邦人搞事！
t_en: You... Fine, I'm not pursuing this matter today. Just don't let me catch that outlander doing anything funny!

t_ch: 这场纠纷结束得过于迅速。人群陷入了七嘴八舌的讨论，有人赞叹于老者的精妙言语，有人则为没热闹可看而感到遗憾。
t_en: This conflict was resolved way too quickly. The crowd is engrossed in gossipy discourse, some lauding the geezer's glibness, others sorry to see the drama end so soon.

t_ch: 哎呀，老先生…多谢，多谢……
t_en: Ah, Mister... Thank you so much...

t_ch: 好了，你也快走吧。记得别再给人留下话柄——哪怕你并没有做错。
t_en: All right, you run along quickly. Don't give anyone else a reason to speak ill of you, even if you did nothing wrong.

t_ch: 慌张的外邦人连连道谢，消失在了人群尽头。
t_en: The flustered outlander expresses profuse appreciation and disappears into the crowd.

t_ch: 这位小友，请不要急着离开。我还有些话要说。
t_en: Don't be in such a hurry to leave, young friend. I still have something to say.

t_ch: 所以，你到底是谁？
t_en: So, who exactly are you?

t_ch: 我名叫苏克提瑞斯，全奥赫玛最无知的老头，也是最虔诚的求知者。
t_en: My name is Soctires, the most witless geezer in Okhema, and a most devoted seeker of knowledge.

t_ch: 你刚才为那外邦人撑腰的举动令我印象深刻，善良的灵魂在哪个城邦都很稀缺。
t_en: Your support for the outlander left a deep impression on me. Kind souls are rare in most cities.

t_ch: 应该的，您过奖了。
t_en: I merely did what I had to do, you flatter me.

t_ch: 哈哈，看来你已经把善举当成了习惯。我认为我们有深入交流的必要。
t_en: Haha, it looks like you're a habitual altruist. I believe it necessary for us to have a deeper conversation.

t_ch: 无所谓，我会出手。
t_en: We have no choice. I will stand in.
